safety: All BallPark facilities carry risks for players and spectators. Flying objects including balls and bats are some of those inherent concerns. We recommend all spectators sit behind our large backstop screens and observation decks and stay aware at all times.
All teams are required to have team insurance and all visitors must sign our park waiver.
If any injuries occur, immediately notify the umpire and the site director. An injury report must be filled out. If an ambulance is needed, we will help facilitate that as soon as possible.
Children should remain supervised by their parent or custodian at all times.
rules: No gum, chewing tobacco, or seeds
No outside coolers, except 1 team cooler
No heating or cooling units of any kind
No bicycles, scooters, roller skates, skateboards, or hoverboards
No public music players or noise makers in the stands, dugouts, or fields
insurance: Each team is required to have proof of insurance with them at all times. Proof must also be provided to Adventure Sports and Entertainment prior to the tournament. The insurance certificate must have no less than $2,000,000 General Aggregate and $1,000,000 for Each Occurrence. The insurance certificate must have Adventure Sports and Entertainment listed as the certificate holder and additionally insured.
umpires: Any foul language, arguing, berating, or unbecoming actions towards any umpire can result in either temporary expulsion or permanent banning from the facility.
equipment: Teams must bring their own practice balls for the batting cage and warm-up. Teams should also bring helmets, bats, gloves, catcher’s gear, first aid, and other equipment needed for the team. No metal cleats.
refundSummary: 45 days or more before an event: full refund if the event is not at max registration.
If the event has reached max registration, no refund will be given unless a replacement team is found.
Once schedules are set to be released on the Monday before the event, there will be no refunds regardless of whether a replacement can be found.
Less than 30 days before the event with a replacement team found prior to 48 hours before the event: 50% refund.
Within 96 hours of the event: no refund regardless of replacement team status.
Weather/event cancellation credits: 75% credit if no games are started, 50% credit if 25% of guaranteed games are started, 25% credit if 50% are started, and no credit if more than 50% are started.
refundDetails: Adventure Sports understands that unforeseen circumstances can occur over the course of a season. Teams should plan for the unexpected and understand that events can sell out weeks or months in advance. Field rental costs, umpire fees, UIC fees, assigning fees, credit card fees, field labor, admin costs, and insurance must be paid despite weather cancellations and in most cases team withdrawals. If governmental agencies shut down play due to a national or state emergency, refunds are not guaranteed and contingencies will be determined at that time. If teams are due a refund but are registered and unpaid for future events run by the same director, that refund may be applied to the balance due on those future events. Adventure Sports reserves the right to adjust this policy at any time.
